name: Federated Auto Parts Raceway at I-55

talkingPoints: Unique parking lot street circuit in Los Angeles

talkingPoints: Jackie Stewart named it "The Green Hell" in 1968
Nearly 13 miles and 154 corners — longer than some airports
Almost 300m of elevation change per lap through the Eifel mountains
The Karussell is a banked concrete bowl originally formed by drivers cutting the corner
Niki Lauda's near-fatal 1976 crash led to F1 leaving the Nordschleife permanently
A yard of the original bricks remains at the start-finish line
Winners traditionally kiss the bricks after winning
The largest single-day sporting event in the world by attendance
Originally surfaced entirely with 3.2 million bricks in 1909

174m elevation change — the mountain section uses narrow public roads

The Bathurst 1000 is effectively Australia's motorsport national holiday

name: Detroit Grand Prix at Belle Isle

talkingPoints: Originally 15km using public roads through the Ardennes forest

Weather can differ between sectors due to the 7km length

One of only a handful of circuits that runs counter-clockwise

Has hosted more F1 races than any other circuit
